act: SEBI (Issue of Capital and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2018
title: General Conditions for Public Issue
content: An issuer making a public issue shall satisfy the following conditions: the issuer, any of its promoters, promoter group, or directors are not debarred from accessing the capital market by SEBI; the issuer is not in the list of wilful defaulters published by the RBI; no petition for winding up has been admitted against the issuer; the issuer has made an application to one or more stock exchanges for listing of specified securities; the issuer has entered into an agreement with a depository for dematerialisation of specified securities; all existing partly paid-up equity shares have been made fully paid-up or forfeited. These conditions ensure that only credible issuers access public markets and protect investor interests.

act: SEBI (Issue of Capital and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2018
title: Eligibility Requirements for IPO
content: An issuer shall be eligible to make an initial public offer only if the issuer has net tangible assets of at least three crore rupees in each of the preceding three full years, of which not more than fifty percent are held in monetary assets; has an average operating profit of at least fifteen crore rupees during any three of the preceding five years; has a net worth of at least one crore rupees in each of the preceding three full years; and the aggregate size of the issue does not exceed five times the pre-issue net worth. If the issuer does not satisfy these criteria, it may still make an IPO if the issue is made through the book building process with at least seventy-five percent of the net offer allocated to qualified institutional buyers.

act: SEBI (Issue of Capital and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2018
title: Pricing - Differential Pricing
content: An issuer may offer specified securities at different prices subject to the conditions that the price at which the specified securities are offered to the applicants in the retail individual investor category shall not be higher than the price at which they are offered to other categories. Differential pricing may also be offered to employees of the issuer. The issuer and the lead manager shall ensure that the basis of pricing is disclosed in the offer document and that investors are given adequate disclosures regarding the pricing methodology. Where the price is determined through the book building process, the issuer may offer a discount of not more than ten percent on the floor price to retail individual investors and eligible employees.

act: SEBI (Issue of Capital and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2018
title: Reservation on Competitive Basis and Allocation
content: An issuer making a public issue may reserve on a competitive basis a portion of the specified securities being issued for allocation to employees of the issuer and, in case of a new issuer, to shareholders of the promoting companies. Reservation for employees shall not exceed five percent of the post-issue capital. Reservation for shareholders of the promoting company shall not exceed ten percent of the issue size. The allotment in respect of the reserved category shall be on a proportionate basis. Any unsubscribed portion in the reserved category shall be added back to the respective category in the net offer to the public.

act: SEBI (Issue of Capital and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2018
title: Minimum Promoters' Contribution
content: The promoters of the issuer shall contribute in the public issue to the extent of not less than twenty percent of the post-issue capital. Where the issuer has been listed for a period of at least three years and has a track record of dividend payment for at least three out of immediately preceding five years, the requirement of minimum promoters' contribution shall not apply. The minimum promoters' contribution shall be brought in at least one day prior to the opening of the issue. The contribution may be by way of equity shares allotted to the promoters at a price not less than the price at which equity shares are offered to the public.

act: SEBI (Issue of Capital and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2018
title: Lock-in Requirements
content: The minimum promoters' contribution including excess promoters' contribution shall be locked in for a period of eighteen months from the date of allotment in the public issue. In case of an offer for sale, the minimum promoters' contribution shall be locked in for a period of eighteen months from the date of allotment in the initial public offer. The entire pre-issue capital of the issuer held by persons other than the promoters shall be locked in for a period of six months from the date of allotment. Specified securities allotted on a preferential basis to any person shall be locked in for a period of eighteen months from the date of trading approval. These lock-in periods are designed to ensure promoter commitment and market stability post-listing.

act: SEBI (Issue of Capital and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2018
title: Innovators Growth Platform - Eligibility
content: An issuer whose shares are proposed to be listed on the Innovators Growth Platform (IGP) shall have at least twenty-five percent of its pre-issue capital held by eligible investors (qualified institutional buyers, family trusts with net worth of more than five hundred crore rupees, accredited investors, or regulated entities) for a period of at least two years prior to the date of filing of the prospectus with SEBI. The issuer shall have a minimum application size of two lakh rupees. The IGP was introduced to facilitate listing of startups and new-age technology companies that may not meet the traditional profitability or net tangible asset requirements for the main board.

act: SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015
title: Principles Governing Disclosures
content: The listed entity shall make disclosures and abide by its obligations under these regulations in letter and spirit taking into consideration the following principles: information shall be prepared and disclosed in accordance with applicable accounting standards and shall give a true and fair picture; the information disclosed shall be adequate and timely; disclosures should be prompt, adequate, and easy to understand; channels for dissemination of information shall provide for equal, timely, and cost-efficient access to relevant information by investors; the listed entity shall ensure that key functions and roles are performed by approved persons. These principles underpin all disclosure requirements and establish the overarching standards of transparency expected from listed companies.

act: SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015
title: Board of Directors
content: The board of directors of a listed entity shall have an optimum combination of executive and non-executive directors with at least one woman director and not less than fifty percent of the board of directors shall comprise non-executive directors. Where the chairperson of the board is a non-executive director, at least one-third of the board shall comprise independent directors, and where the chairperson is an executive director or a promoter, at least half of the board shall comprise independent directors. The board shall meet at least four times a year with a maximum time gap of one hundred and twenty days between any two consecutive meetings. The quorum for a meeting of the board shall be one-third of its total strength or three directors, whichever is higher.

act: SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015
title: Disclosure of Events or Information
content: Every listed entity shall make disclosures of any events or information which in the opinion of the board of directors of the listed entity is material. Events which are deemed material include acquisition, merger, defrauding, change in directors or key managerial personnel, revision in credit rating, outcome of board meetings, agreements which would impact management or control, and fraud or defaults by promoters or key managerial personnel. The listed entity shall first disclose the event or information to the stock exchange and shall not disclose the same to any person or group of persons ahead of such disclosure. The disclosure shall be made as soon as reasonably possible and not later than twenty-four hours from the occurrence of the event or information.

act: SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015
title: Shareholding Pattern
content: The listed entity shall submit to the stock exchange a statement showing the holding of securities and shareholding pattern separately for each class of securities within the prescribed time period. The statement shall be submitted on a quarterly basis within twenty-one days from the end of each quarter. The statement shall include details of promoter and promoter group shareholding, public shareholding, shares held by custodians and against which depository receipts have been issued, and the total shareholding as a percentage of total number of shares. Changes in shareholding of promoters and top ten shareholders shall also be disclosed within specified timelines.

act: SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015
title: Financial Results
content: The listed entity shall submit to the stock exchange quarterly and year-to-date standalone financial results within forty-five days of end of each quarter other than the last quarter. Annual audited standalone financial results shall be submitted within sixty days from the end of the financial year. The listed entity having subsidiaries shall also submit consolidated financial results. The financial results shall be approved by the board of directors or a committee thereof. If the listed entity has a subsidiary that is not a company, the consolidated financial results shall be filed based on the last audited financial statements of the subsidiary. Limited review or audit report shall accompany the financial results.

act: SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015
title: Website Disclosures
content: The listed entity shall maintain a functional website containing basic information about the listed entity including details of its business, terms and conditions of appointment of independent directors, composition of various committees of the board, code of conduct, details of establishment of vigil mechanism or whistle blower policy, criteria of making payments to non-executive directors, policy on dealing with related party transactions, policy for determining material subsidiaries, details of familiarisation programmes imparted to independent directors, contact information of the designated officials for the purpose of investor grievance resolution, email address for grievance redressal, and financial information including complete copy of annual reports.

act: SEBI (Substantial Acquisition of Shares and Takeovers) Regulations, 2011
title: Open Offer Trigger - Substantial Acquisition
content: No acquirer shall acquire shares or voting rights in a target company which taken together with shares or voting rights, if any, held by him and by persons acting in concert with him in such target company, entitle them to exercise twenty-five percent or more of the voting rights in such target company, unless the acquirer makes a public announcement of an open offer for acquiring shares of such target company. This threshold trigger is fundamental to takeover regulations and ensures that any person acquiring a substantial stake must provide an exit opportunity to existing shareholders. The open offer must be for at least twenty-six percent of the total shares of the target company.

act: SEBI (Substantial Acquisition of Shares and Takeovers) Regulations, 2011
title: Open Offer Trigger - Creeping Acquisition
content: No acquirer who together with persons acting in concert with him has acquired and holds shares or voting rights entitling them to exercise twenty-five percent or more of the voting rights in a target company but less than the maximum permissible non-public shareholding, shall acquire within any financial year additional shares or voting rights in such target company entitling them to exercise more than five percent of the voting rights, unless the acquirer makes a public announcement of an open offer for acquiring shares of such target company. This provision prevents gradual acquisition of controlling stakes without triggering an open offer obligation, thereby protecting minority shareholder interests.

title: Communication or Procurement of Unpublished Price Sensitive Information
content: No insider shall communicate, provide, or allow access to any unpublished price sensitive information (UPSI) relating to a company or securities listed or proposed to be listed, to any person including other insiders, except where such communication is in furtherance of legitimate purposes, performance of duties, or discharge of legal obligations. Unpublished price sensitive information means any information relating to a company or its securities, directly or indirectly, that is not generally available which upon becoming generally available is likely to materially affect the price of the securities. This includes financial results, dividends, change in capital structure, mergers and acquisitions, changes in key managerial personnel, and material events.

title: Trading When in Possession of UPSI
content: No insider shall trade in securities that are listed or proposed to be listed on a stock exchange when in possession of unpublished price sensitive information. The trading shall be presumed to have been motivated by the knowledge and awareness of such information in the insider's possession unless the insider proves otherwise. Defences available include trades pursuant to a trading plan approved by the compliance officer, off-market inter-se transfers between insiders who were in possession of the same UPSI, and transactions carried out through the block deal window mechanism between persons who were in possession of the UPSI. The regulations apply to all persons who are in possession of or have access to UPSI, not just designated insiders.
